title: A Fake Artist Goes to New York

subtopics.title: What's in the Box
subtopics.content:
#### Player-Count Reference
- **Standard game** — 5 to 10 players.
- **Recommended** — 7 players for best deduction dynamics.

#### Core Components
- **Color markers** — 10 colored drawing markers (one per player).
- **Category cards** — Cards for the Question Master to write the category and word.
- **Drawing canvas** — 1 shared drawing sheet or pad.
- **Marker caps** — Color-coded caps to identify each player's strokes.

#### Card Details
- **Word cards** — Small cards dealt to each artist; all show the secret word except the Fake Artist's card which shows an "X".
- **Question Master card** — Identifies the QM role.

subtopics.title: Board Layout
subtopics.content:
#### Setup Sequence
1. **Choose Question Master** — One player becomes the Question Master (QM) for the round.
2. **Select category and word** — QM secretly picks a category (e.g., "food") and a specific word within that category (e.g., "pizza").
3. **Prepare cards** — QM writes the category and word on cards for each player. One card gets an "X" instead of the word.
4. **Distribute cards** — Shuffle and deal one card face-down to each artist. No one reveals their card.
5. **Place canvas** — Put the shared drawing surface in the center where all players can reach it.
6. **Assign markers** — Each player takes a unique colored marker.

#### Table Layout
- **Central canvas** — Drawing sheet in the middle of the table.
- **QM position** — QM sits where they can observe all drawings and manage the round.
- **Player seating** — Artists sit around the canvas with their assigned markers.

subtopics.title: Player Setup
subtopics.content:
#### Role Assignment
- **Question Master** — 1 player; does not draw. Picks the category and word, prepares cards.
- **Real Artists** — All other players except one; they know the secret word.
- **Fake Artist** — 1 player (determined randomly by receiving the "X" card); does not know the word.

#### Information Access
- **QM** — Knows the word and knows who the Fake Artist is.
- **Real Artists** — Know the word but do not know who the Fake Artist is.
- **Fake Artist** — Knows only the category (visible on the card), not the specific word.

subtopics.title: Starting the Game
subtopics.content:
#### Pre-First-Turn Checks
- **Card secrecy** — Each player has looked at their card privately.
- **QM announces category** — QM announces the category aloud (e.g., "The category is: animals").
- **Marker check** — Every artist has a unique colored marker.

#### First Action
1. **QM picks starting player** — QM chooses who draws first (often the player to QM's left).
2. **First stroke** — The starting player adds exactly one continuous line or stroke to the canvas.
3. **Play continues clockwise** — Each player adds one stroke in turn order.

subtopics.title: Turn Structure
subtopics.content:
#### Drawing Rounds
- **Two full rounds** — Play goes around the table exactly twice (each artist draws exactly 2 strokes total).
- **One stroke per turn** — On your turn, add exactly one continuous line or stroke to the shared drawing.
- **No lifting** — Each stroke must be one continuous pen-down motion (you may not lift and re-place).
- **Marker color** — Use only your assigned colored marker so contributions are identifiable.

#### Round Flow
1. **Round 1** — Starting from the chosen player, go clockwise. Each artist adds one stroke.
2. **Round 2** — Continue from the same starting player for a second pass. Each artist adds one more stroke.
3. **Proceed to voting** — After all players have drawn twice, move to the voting phase.

subtopics.title: Actions & Options
subtopics.content:
#### Drawing Actions
- **Add a stroke** — Draw one continuous line, shape, or mark on the canvas.
- **Placement** — You may draw anywhere on the canvas, connecting to or separate from existing strokes.
- **No erasing** — You cannot erase or modify existing strokes.
- **No writing** — You may not write letters or words.

#### Voting Phase
- **Discussion** — After both drawing rounds, players may briefly discuss who they suspect is the Fake Artist.
- **Simultaneous vote** — On the count of three, all artists (including the Fake Artist) point at who they think is the Fake Artist.
- **Majority rules** — The player with the most votes is accused. Ties require a re-vote between tied players.
- **QM does not vote** — The Question Master does not participate in voting.

subtopics.title: Special Mechanics
subtopics.content:
#### Fake Artist's Defense
- **If caught (majority vote)** — The Fake Artist gets one chance to guess the specific word. If correct, the Fake Artist and QM still win. If wrong, the Real Artists win.
- **Word guess specificity** — The Fake Artist must name the exact word, not just the category.

#### Win Conditions by Outcome
- **Fake Artist not caught** — Fake Artist and QM win together. Real Artists lose.
- **Fake Artist caught + guesses word correctly** — Fake Artist and QM win. Real Artists lose.
- **Fake Artist caught + guesses word incorrectly** — Real Artists win. Fake Artist and QM lose.

#### QM Alliance
- **QM wins/loses with the Fake Artist** — The Question Master is aligned with the Fake Artist for scoring purposes.
- **QM strategy** — QM should choose words that are not too easy (artists draw obviously) and not too hard (no one can draw it).

#### Drawing Etiquette
- **Speed** — Do not take too long on your stroke; deliberate stalling draws suspicion.
- **Poker face** — Avoid reacting to others' strokes or revealing information verbally.

subtopics.title: Endgame & Scoring
subtopics.content:
#### Round Resolution
- **Vote result** — After the vote, resolve the outcome per the win condition rules.
- **Reveal** — The Fake Artist reveals their "X" card. If accused correctly, they attempt the word guess.

#### Multi-Round Play
- **Rotate QM** — The QM role passes to the next player each round.
- **Optional scoring** — Track wins across multiple rounds. Real Artists each earn 1 point for a win. Fake Artist earns 2 points for a win. QM earns 1 point when the Fake Artist wins.
- **Game end** — Play a set number of rounds (often one round per player so everyone is QM once). Most points wins.

#### Scoring Model
- **Primary model** — Elimination-style. Each round has a winning side and a losing side.
- **No VP track** — The base game does not use victory points or cumulative scoring.

subtopics.title: Opening Priorities
subtopics.content:
#### As a Real Artist
- **Draw recognizably but not too obviously** — Your stroke should show you know the word without spelling it out for the Fake Artist.
- **Start with general features** — Draw a broad structural element first (outline, key shape) rather than a defining detail.
- **Watch others** — Pay attention to hesitation or vague strokes from other players.

#### As the Fake Artist
- **Observe first strokes carefully** — Use early strokes from others to deduce the word.
- **Draw confidently** — Hesitation is the biggest tell. Commit to your stroke quickly.
- **Stay general** — Add strokes that could plausibly relate to many words in the announced category.

subtopics.title: Common Mistakes
subtopics.content:
#### Frequent Errors
- **Being too specific** — Real Artists who draw an unmistakable defining feature give the Fake Artist all the information they need.
- **Being too vague** — Real Artists who draw nothing meaningful look suspicious and may get voted out.
- **Copying others** — The Fake Artist who just extends someone else's stroke pattern is often caught by attentive players.
- **Forgetting the QM alliance** — Players who forget the QM also wins with the Fake Artist may not scrutinize the QM's word choice.
- **Delayed voting** — Overlong discussion before voting allows the Fake Artist to build a convincing narrative.

subtopics.title: Key Decisions
subtopics.content:
#### Decision 1: How Much to Reveal (Real Artist)
- **Reveal more** — Helps prove your innocence but risks giving the Fake Artist enough info to guess the word.
- **Reveal less** — Protects the word but risks being suspected as the Fake Artist.

#### Decision 2: When to Commit (Fake Artist)
- **Early commitment** — Drawing something specific early is risky but looks confident.
- **Late adaptation** — Waiting to see more strokes helps accuracy but your first stroke may look aimless.

#### Decision 3: Word Difficulty (QM)
- **Easy word** — Artists draw clearly, making the Fake Artist easy to spot. Bad for QM's team.
- **Hard word** — Artists struggle to draw it, giving the Fake Artist cover. Good for QM's team but may lead to a poor drawing experience.
- **Medium word** — Best balance. Something drawable but with room for ambiguity.
